headline: Large Energy User Grid Cost Shifting
short_summary: This bill mandates large energy users, such as data centers, to bear the costs of increased electric generating capacity, thereby reducing the burden on residential ratepayers.
status: Last Action: Signed by President on 2026-04-22
supporters: Residential ratepayers, consumer advocacy groups, Dominion Energy.
opponents: Data center industry, large industrial energy users.
news_headline: Virginia bill aims to cut residential energy costs by shifting expenses to data centers
news_summary: A Virginia bill, similar to HB 1393, aims to reduce residential electricity bills by shifting grid upgrade and capacity costs to data centers and other large energy users. This could save average households over $5 per month while increasing data center rates by approximately 15.8%.

headline: Data Center Water Use Reporting
short_summary: This bill mandates water providers to data centers to report monthly water consumption data, including reclaimed water, to the State Water Control Board for transparency and planning.
status: Last Action: Approved by Governor-Chapter 896 (Effective 1/1/2027) on 2026-04-13
supporters: Environmental groups, water conservationists, local communities, State Water Control Board.
news_headline: Virginia adds new restrictions, including tracking data center water usage
news_summary: Virginia Governor Abigail Spanberger signed bills, including SB 553, to regulate data centers by tracking their water consumption. This legislation aims to provide transparency on the environmental impact of these facilities, which use significant amounts of water for cooling.

headline: Data Center Siting and Noise Limits
short_summary: This bill establishes a permit process for large data centers, requiring sound assessments near residential areas and schools, and grants localities more authority to consider environmental and historical impacts during siting.
status: Last Action: Incorporated by Counties, Cities and Towns (HB153-Thomas) (Voice Vote) on 2026-01-23
supporters: Local communities, residents near data center sites, environmental groups.
opponents: Data center developers, real estate industry.
news_headline: Virginia adds new restrictions, including efforts to reduce noise from data centers
news_summary: Virginia Governor Abigail Spanberger signed bills, including those related to data center siting and noise limits, to regulate the rapidly growing tech facilities. This legislation aims to address concerns from local communities regarding the impact of data centers on their surroundings.
